substrates.stoichiometry: 2 equiv thiol → 1 equiv disulfide (symmetrical); 1 equiv aromatic thiol + 5 equiv aliphatic thiol (unsymmetrical)

chemistry.scope_summary: Aromatic thiols (benzyl mercaptan, thiophenol, 4-methoxythiophenol, 4-chlorothiophenol, 4-bromothiophenol, 3-trifluoromethylthiophenol), cysteine derivatives, peptides (Oxytocin, y1fatc peptide). Symmetrical disulfides in 72-96% yield (batch) and 60-99% yield (flow). Unsymmetrical aryl-alkyl disulfides in 23-86% yield with good selectivity.

optimization_table.key_findings: Carbonate buffer in CH3CN (1:3) with white LED gave 28% yield

figures.caption: Scheme 1.  Comparison of flow and batch yields for symmetrical disulfide formation. Reaction conditions in batch: thiol (1 mmol), TMEDA (1 mmol), TiO 2  (AeroxideÒ P25, 12.5 mol%), EtOH (2 mL), O 2 , visible light irradiation. The yields reported are isolated. For the detailed reaction conditions in flow see the Supporting Information.

figures.key_information: Photocatalytic oxidative coupling of benzyl mercaptan using TiO2 catalyst under O2 atmosphere with TMEDA and EtOH as solvents, light irradiation indicated by lamp symbol
figures.caption: Scheme 1.  Comparison of flow and batch yields for symmetrical disulfide formation. Reaction conditions in batch: thiol (1 mmol), TMEDA (1 mmol), TiO 2  (AeroxideÒ P25, 12.5 mol%), EtOH (2 mL), O 2 , visible light irradiation. The yields reported are isolated. For the detailed reaction conditions in flow see the Supporting Information.

Scheme 2.  Scope of unsymmetrical disulfides. Reaction conditions in batch: (hetero)aromatic thiol (1 mmol), aliphatic thiol (5 mmol), TMEDA (6 mmol), TiO 2  (AeroxideÒ P25, 12.5 mol%), EtOH (2 mL), O 2 , visible light irradiation. figures.key_information: Oxidative cyclization reaction converting compound 18 to compound 19 using O2 and TiO2 catalyst in phosphate buffer
figures.caption: Scheme 5.  Intramolecular disulfide formation yielding the native form of yeast-derived y1fatc peptide  18 . Reaction conditions: precursor  18  (0.25 mg, 0.13  m mol), TiO 2  (AeroxideÒ P25, 1 mg), phosphate buffer (0.25 mL, 20 m m ), milliQ water (0.25 mL), O 2 , visible light irradiation, 1 h.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Long reaction times (8 h batch vs 5 min flow) and limited reaction efficiency
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Substantial rate acceleration from 8 h to 5 min in continuous-flow packed-bed reactor
translation_logic.what_changed: Reactor design to packed-bed continuous flow with improved light penetration
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: TiO2 photocatalyst, TMEDA base, O2 atmosphere, visible light irradiation
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Improved handling of O2 atmosphere in continuous flow system

figure_descriptions: Reactor schemes showing photocatalytic oxidative coupling setup with TiO2 catalyst and light irradiation
Scope tables showing yields for symmetrical (60-99%) and unsymmetrical (23-86%) disulfides
Mechanistic schemes for peptide disulfide formation including Oxytocin synthesis

notes: High confidence extraction with clear batch-to-flow translation showing 96x time reduction (480 min to 5 min). Multiple scope tables and reactor schemes provide comprehensive view of the photocatalytic disulfide formation process.
